_comment: M11 size ceilings — minified + gzip (bytes). Guards against accidental bloat while the framework is still being built; bump a limit deliberately when a feature legitimately grows it. runtime-full is the WHOLE runtime+dom surface bundled together (NOT what an app ships — that's counter-app, kept tight). It was the plan's ~3.6 kB class at M11; Phase A added context, onMount, lazy, @defer, and error boundaries (bumped to 4400). Phase B.9 added bindShow (v-show) + dynElement (<w:element>), nudging it past 4400, so it was bumped to 5120; then B.10 @key + B.11 tick + B.12 defineCustomElement + B.13 transition (intro/outro coordination + removeWithOutro) grew it to ~5.18 kB, so it was bumped to 5632 (the remaining Phase B work — file-based routing, Link prefetch — is in the router package, not runtime-full). reactive-core grew 1024->1126 because error-boundary routing in run() is on the core hot path (not tree-shakeable). counter-app stays the real-world guard — everything Phase B tree-shakes out when unused (still 1.29 kB).
